properties.mechanism: Unsubscribes at a given position remove those recipients from subsequent steps, directly reducing the next step's recipient count and increasing drop-off.
Positions with low open/click rates tend to co-occur with higher drop-off as disengaged recipients leave the sequence, so the two move together by position.
Declining open/click rates over a sequence statistically co-move with rising step-to-step drop-off as engagement falls.
Performance by Position is computed by averaging the per-email Open Rate and Click Rate values (the Performance Trend metrics) across all emails sharing the same Nth position.
Weak open/click performance at a given sequence position signals poor relevance or fatigue, which drives more recipients to unsubscribe at that same position.
Low engagement (open/click) at a given position reduces recipients who continue, increasing the drop-off rate from that step to the next.
Declining open and click engagement signals message fatigue, causing more recipients to unsubscribe and raising the unsubscribe rate.
Stronger open and click engagement keeps recipients active in the flow, reducing the share who drop off between sequence steps.

Both are positional reception metrics for the Nth email, so positions where engagement (open/click) declines tend to coincide with elevated unsubscribe rates due to shared audience fatigue/relevance effects.

Sync Status by Table computes Minutes Since Sync (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - MAX(_FIVETRAN_SYNCED)) per table, which is exactly the per-schema minutes_since_sync value that Average Sync Lag sums and divides by schema count.
